master_row.title: Trawinistic Manifold
master_row.canonical_statement: A Trawinistic manifold is a smooth manifold $(M,g)$ equipped with a Lorentzian metric $g$ such that $\det g|_p = 0$ at the TZP and $g$ is non-degenerate elsewhere.
master_row.canonical_equation:
(M,g)\ 	ext{is Trawinistic} \quad \Longleftrightarrow \quad \det g\vert_{p}=0 \ 	ext{and}\ \det g(x)
eq 0 \ 	ext{for}\ x
eq p || g^{-1}(x)\ 	ext{exists for}\ x
eq p, \qquad g^{-1}\vert_{p}\ 	ext{does not exist}
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an algebraic definition relating g, x, p.
master_row.dictionary: For any closed loop () encircling the TZP in (M), there exists a homotopy that deforms () into a lemniscate (figure‑eight) whose self‑intersection occurs at (p).
master_row.encyclopedia: The TZP is marked by metric degeneracy. This is the first mathematical signature that ordinary geometry fails at the zero point and must be replaced by a Trawinistic structure. Interpretive role: This definition gives later IDs a place to live: loops can encircle the TZP, dimensions can branch there, and time or field quantities can acquire singular corrections while remaining well-defined off the anchor. Equation grounding: The local equation anchors are 'latex': 'For any closed loop $$ encircling the TZP in $$, there exists a homotopy that deforms $$ into a lemniscate (figure-eight) whose self-intersection occurs at $p$.', 'mathml': '', 'wolfram': '', 'canonical': 'For any closed loop $$ encircling the TZP in $$, there exists a homotopy that deforms $$ into a lemniscate (figure-eight) whose self-intersection occurs at $p$.'.
